commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human review is needed because the remaining blocker is real-environment proof and auth-provider acceptance, not a concrete code defect for automation.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain concern found; the patch is MiMo-scoped, filters known cookie names/domains, avoids value logging, and bounds session-file parsing. (sha=084a45a32fe5da2d3ab36441749000951847ed60)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a MiMo-specific Firefox sessionstore jsonlz4 cookie fallback, a logging category, a changelog note, and focused MiMo importer tests.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main supports Firefox for MiMo but only builds sessions from persisted query results, so a Firefox profile with no matching auth rows cannot be recovered from session restore. I did not run a live browser-cookie probe because the repository policy warns those checks can trigger macOS prompts.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the remaining blocker is real-environment proof and auth-provider acceptance, not a concrete code defect for automation.

target.title: Fix MiMo Firefox session cookie import
target.body:
## Summary

- recover MiMo authentication cookies from Firefox session-restore files when `cookies.sqlite` has no matching authentication rows
- reuse SweetCookieKit's discovered Gecko stores instead of maintaining separate profile discovery
- prefer active session-restore cookies over stale persisted values for the same domain/name/path
- bound session file input, LZ4 output, JSON traversal, and recovered cookie count
- add focused import-path, merge, format-variant, malformed-input, and resource-limit coverage

## Root cause

Firefox keeps MiMo's active authentication values as session cookies. SweetCookieKit correctly discovers the Firefox profile, but its domain query omits stores with zero matching persisted rows. The prior MiMo importer therefore had no profile from which to inspect `sessionstore-backups/*.jsonlz4`.

## Verification

Exact head: `084a45a32fe5da2d3ab36441749000951847ed60`

- `swift test --filter MiMoProviderTests`: 41 tests passed
- `make check`: passed; SwiftFormat clean and SwiftLint reported zero violations
- autoreview: clean (0.86), no accepted/actionable findings
- contributor reproduction: real Firefox profile exposed only a persisted preference row while active MiMo auth cookies were present in session restore
- local live boundary: this Mac has no Firefox app or profile; the exact CLI reached the existing Chrome Safe Storage authorization prompt instead, so it was stopped without credential entry and no local account success is claimed

## Safety

- only known MiMo cookie names on `xiaomimimo.com` are accepted
- cookie values are never logged
- compressed input is capped at 64 MiB; decoded output at 128 MiB
- JSON traversal and recovered record counts are bounded
- malformed or oversized restore files fail best-effort and fall through to the next candidate file
target.author: aaronflorey
